dashboard.importNotArray: The file must contain a JSON array

spotlight.searchPlaceholder: Search for a password, a login or an account

accountForm.totpHelp: On the website, enable 2FA and click “Enter manually” to get this key.

importModal.title: Import
importModal.label: Paste or type a JSON array of accounts
importModal.textLabel: Paste the content of your .txt file
importModal.textHint: One line per account — Title : email / username (optional) / password. Sections (A-, B-...) are ignored.
importModal.emptyError: The field is empty. Paste or type your content.
importModal.notArrayError: Invalid format. Make sure it's a valid JSON array.
importModal.noEntries: No account detected. Check the file format.

audit.title: Vault analysis
audit.allGood: Your vault is in perfect shape — no issues found.
audit.issuesFound_one: {{count}} issue found — fix it to strengthen your security.
audit.issuesFound_other: {{count}} issues found — fix them to strengthen your security.

background.title: The app will keep running in the background.
background.description: You can reopen it anytime from the icon near the clock or with your keyboard shortcut

settings.title: Settings
settings.exportDesc: Generate a backup file containing all your passwords in plain text. You can use this file to restore your vault on another computer. Keep this file in a safe place!
settings.languageDesc: Choose the display language for RootPass.
settings.shortcutsDesc: Customize the global shortcut that opens the mini launcher (Spotlight) from anywhere on your computer.
settings.comboError: Use Ctrl/Cmd or Alt in the combination.
settings.2faDesc: RootPass generates the 6-digit codes (TOTP) directly, with no external app.
2faSteps: On the website to protect, enable 2FA.
Choose “Enter manually” (or “Show key”) instead of scanning the QR code.
Copy the secret key shown (e.g. JBSWY3DPEHPK3PXP).
In RootPass, open the account → “2FA secret key” field → paste the key.
The 6-digit code appears live in the dashboard, refreshed every 30 s.
settings.2faMigrateTitle: Migrate from Google Authenticator or Authy
settings.2faMigrateDesc: These apps don't let you export secret keys directly. For each account you want to migrate: disable 2FA on the website, re-enable it, and save the secret key in RootPass during setup. For new accounts, use RootPass from the start.
settings.2faSecureDesc: TOTP secret keys are encrypted with AES-256-GCM, just like your passwords. They never leave your machine.
settings.autoLockDesc: Idle time before the vault locks itself.
settings.autoStartDesc: Launches RootPass in the background when Windows starts.

sync.title: Sync
sync.conflictsDesc: Your cloud service created conflict copies because two PCs wrote at the same time. The active file (vault_V2.db) is kept; the copies below will be deleted.
sync.keepAndDelete: Keep vault_V2.db and delete the conflicts
sync.switchedWarning: Vault switched. Warning: {{host}} may have this vault open.
sync.cloudDesc: Point the vault to a OneDrive, Dropbox or Google Drive folder. Sync is automatic between your PCs. The cloud only ever sees AES-256 encrypted binary.
cloudSteps: Install and sign in to a cloud client (OneDrive, Dropbox…) on your PCs.
Click “Change folder” → pick a folder inside your cloud (e.g. OneDrive\RootPass).
Wait for the sync (green icon on the cloud client) before moving to the next PC.
On every other PC: same steps, same folder. RootPass switches to the existing vault.
Use only one PC at a time to avoid conflicts.
sync.usbDesc: Store the vault directly on a USB drive, 100% offline, zero cloud. The database stays AES-256 encrypted even if you lose the drive.
usbSteps: Click “Change folder” → pick a folder on the drive.
On the other PCs: plug in the same drive and point to the same folder.
sync.usbWarning: The drive must stay plugged in while in use. Removing it can corrupt your backups.
sync.encDesc: An encrypted snapshot of your vault. To migrate to a new PC or keep an offline backup, protected by your master password.
sync.vsCloudUsbDesc: Cloud and USB sync the raw SQLite database. The .enc file is schema-independent and works on a fresh install.
sync.vsJsonDesc: JSON exports in plain text. The .enc file is fully encrypted, unreadable without the master password.
sync.encExportDesc: Generates rootpass-backup.enc, protected by your master password.
sync.encImportDesc: Loads a .enc file from another PC. Accounts are added without overwriting existing ones.
encSteps: Click “Export encrypted” and enter your master password.
Transfer the .enc file to the other PC (USB drive, email, cloud…).
On the other PC: Settings → Sync → Encrypted export → “Import encrypted”.
Enter the master password from the source PC.
sync.jsonDesc: Unencrypted export, passwords in plain text. Use it only to migrate to another manager or for a readable backup.
sync.jsonExportDesc: Generates rootpass-export.json with all your accounts in plain text.
sync.jsonImportDescSuffix: button in the dashboard (top right). Accounts are added without overwriting existing ones.
jsonSteps: Transfer the file to the other PC (USB drive, cloud…).
On the other PC: use “Import JSON” in the dashboard.
sync.jsonWarningSuffix: . Delete the file after importing and don't leave it lying around.
sync.vaultMaybeOpenDesc: The computer {{host}} seems to have this vault open. Avoid editing your passwords at the same time.
sync.pwModalDescExport: Enter your master password to encrypt the export file.
sync.pwModalDescImport: Enter the master password of the source vault (the one from the original PC).

help.title: Help
help.subtitle: Frequently asked questions and ways to reach out. RootPass stays 100% local: nothing is sent online.

faq.a: Your vault is encrypted with AES-256-GCM. The key is derived from your master password using PBKDF2 (600,000 iterations, SHA-256). The master password is never stored: it only regenerates the key each time you unlock.
In a local database file (vault_V2.db) on your computer, never on a server. You can choose its location in Settings → Sync.

faq.q: What if I forget my master password?
faq.a: It cannot be recovered. Since no data is stored anywhere else, the vault stays permanently undecryptable without it. That's the price of full security: pick a strong but memorable password, and keep backups.
Never. RootPass is a 100% offline app: it makes no network requests. Your passwords never leave your machine.

faq.q: How do I back up or sync my vault?
faq.a: Through Settings → Sync: a cloud folder (encrypted), a USB drive, or an encrypted .enc export to safely migrate to another PC.

help.githubDesc: The best place to ask a question or report a bug.
help.securityDesc: Private report: do not open a public issue for a vulnerability.
help.privacyNote: No data sent. RootPass is 100% local.

update.readyDescription: Version {{version}} will be installed on restart.
